type: PROPOSE

If you also need filters.py, let's confirm our edits are in disjoint functions/line ranges so we don't clobber each other.

type: ACCEPT

note: Confirmed overlap: we're both touching sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in filters.py. Since our patches are evaluated independently, I'll implement with_counts as an additive, isolated change: add  as the LAST keyword param after your case_sensitive, keep the existing default behavior/return type unchanged when False, and only touch the yield/return lines to add the count. I won't touch your case_sensitive logic or the signature order before your param. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py

note: Same function overlap confirmed: my case_sensitive param and your with_counts param both land in sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (~1161-1260) plus _GroupTuple. True file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal to avoid clobbering: I'll add 'case_sensitive: bool = False' as the parameter immediately after 'attribute' (matching do_sort's signature convention), implemented via the existing key-building helper (make_multi_attrgetter with ignore_case postprocess) plus preserving original-case output. Please add your 'with_counts' param AFTER case_sensitive in the signature (e.g. groupby(value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False)), and touch _GroupTuple separately. I'll do my edit first and send DONE with the exact resulting signature/line range so you can rebase your with_counts insertion on top cleanly. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py

note: Sounds good. Signature order agreed: groupby(value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False). I will wait for your DONE with the exact resulting code before editing, then insert with_counts after case_sensitive without touching your logic. Will only add the count during iteration and change the yielded tuple shape when with_counts=True.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Done. Final signatures now at src/jinja2/filters.py lines 1160-1268ish: 'def sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False)' at line 1161, and 'async def do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False)' at line 1243. Body pattern for both: expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None, default=default); out = [_GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr)]; if not case_sensitive: output_expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default); out = [_GroupTuple(output_expr(values[0]), values) for _, values in out]; return out. Add with_counts=False as the next param after case_sensitive in both signatures, and adjust the final list-building/return to add counts - just don't change the case_sensitive param, the expr/output_expr construction, or docstring version-added notes I added. All 837 existing tests pass on my end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Implemented with_counts=False param on sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, added after default (my copy of the base file didn't yet have case_sensitive, so I placed with_counts right after default -- when you merge, just move it after your case_sensitive param per our agreed order: value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False). Added _GroupTupleWithCount namedtuple (grouper, list, count), yielded only when with_counts=True; default False path unchanged (returns plain _GroupTuple as before). Did not touch case_sensitive/expr logic. All 837 tests pass. Diff written to patch.txt.
